message:
fix: StringArray.slice negative step bug + conformance tests

Fix bug where s[::-1] would produce "olle" instead of "olleh" because
the negative step logic excluded index 0 when stop was None.

Changes:
- Use Option<usize> for stop_idx to distinguish "stop at index 0" from
  "iterate to beginning inclusive"
- When stop is None and step < 0, use stop_bound=-1 to include index 0
- Add 8 new string slice conformance test cases (basic, from_start,
  to_end, negative_indices, with_step, reverse, reverse_step2, unicode)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -916,6 +916,12 @@ struct StringDifferentialCase {
     #[serde(default)]
     repeat: Option<usize>,
     #[serde(default)]
+    slice_start: Option<isize>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    slice_stop: Option<isize>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    slice_step: Option<isize>,
+    #[serde(default)]
     expected_values: Vec<f64>,
     #[serde(default)]
     expected_strings: Vec<String>,
@@ -6740,6 +6746,10 @@ fn execute_string_differential_operation(
                     .to_vec(),
             ))
         }
+        "slice" => {
+            let result = lhs.slice(case.slice_start, case.slice_stop, case.slice_step);
+            Ok(as_text(result.values().to_vec()))
+        }
         other => Err(StringSuiteError::new(
             "string_policy_unknown_operation",
             format!("unsupported string differential operation {other}"),
